title: 1981-04-16_Pat_82.txt
key_claims: Dick discovers that Advaita Vedanta (Shankara) provides the precise framework for Ditheon: we have an empirical ego, but underneath it is "Atman" (pure consciousness), which is identical to "Brahman" (the absolute ground of reality).

The meta-abstraction is therefore seeing Shankara's *Tat Tvam Asi* (Thou art that). The "one mother seen twice" realization applies to the inner self and the outer world sharing a single conscious essence.

In *The Transmigration of Timothy Archer*, the transferable psyche joining Bill's mind is not just a human ghost, but Christ (the ubiquitous divine unitary psyche). Thus, Ditheon is the human psyche + God.
"Valis" is Brahman, seen externally, camouflaged as the universe of plurality. Recognizing it breaks the fetters of Karma and time, achieving Moksha.
recurring_concepts: **Transferable Psyches**: The mechanics of Ditheon involve a divine psyche overlaying a human one, allowing temporal parallaxis and the breaking of causality.
**Moksha**: Sudden realization/enlightenment that frees one from the karmic wheel.
reading_excerpt: Dick discovers that Advaita Vedanta (Shankara) provides the precise framework for Ditheon: we have an empirical ego, but underneath it is "Atman" (pure consciousness), which is identical to "Brahman" (the absolute ground of reality).
